nativeRange.habitat: Seaward reefs in areas of moderate to rich coral growth; typically perches at the bases of coral heads on hard bottoms at depths of 1–52 m

diet.notes: Generally considered an unfussy ambush carnivore that has been reported to readily accept marine meats, frozen preparations (mysis shrimp, krill, chopped clam/shrimp/squid), and live feeder shrimp. In the wild it has been documented feeding roughly equally on small fishes, shrimps, and crabs, with smaller amounts of sipunculids and zooplankton. Generally advised to offer pigment-rich and varied foods to help maintain its bright red coloration, which has been reported to fade in captivity on a monotonous diet.

summary: A small, bright-red Indo-Pacific hawkfish with bluish-white facial markings and faint vertical body bars; generally considered hardy and beginner-friendly but notably aggressive toward small tankmates and ornamental crustaceans.
careNotes:
The Blood Red Hawkfish (Cirrhitops fasciatus, also widely sold as Redbarred, Redbar, or Banded Hawkfish) is a small reef-associated hawkfish from the Indo-Pacific, with confirmed populations in Madagascar, Mauritius, Reunion, Japan, and Hawaii. In the wild it tends to perch motionlessly at the base of coral heads, ambushing passing prey — a hunting style that translates directly into tank behavior, where it has been reported to readily pick off small shrimp, crabs, and small fish tankmates.

It is generally considered a hardy beginner-level species in terms of water-quality tolerance and feeding response, but its aggressive temperament is a separate dimension that needs planning for. Tankmates should ideally be larger than the hawkfish and at least semi-aggressive; small peaceful fish, ornamental shrimp, and small crabs are at significant risk of predation. Keeping more than one Cirrhitops fasciatus per tank is generally not recommended due to conspecific aggression. Reef compatibility is widely rated as 'with caution' — corals and sessile invertebrates are typically left alone, but mobile crustaceans are fair game.

Like other members of the family Cirrhitidae, this species is widely understood to be a protogynous (female-first) sequential hermaphrodite that forms haremic social groups in the wild, with a dominant male presiding over multiple females. Captive pair or harem keeping has been reported, though specific breeding accounts for C. fasciatus are scarce in the hobby literature.

Cross-source spread: Adult size — LiveAquaria 5 in (12.7 cm), AquariumDomain 5 in (12.7 cm), FishBase 12.7 cm TL, reefapp 13 cm; converged on ~10–13 cm. Temperature — LiveAquaria 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C), AquariumDomain 72–80°F (22.2–26.7°C), tankfacts 76–80°F (24.4–26.7°C), reefapp ~26°C, FishBase wild observations 26.2–26.7°C; averaged to 23–27°C. Salinity — LiveAquaria and AquariumDomain 1.020–1.025 SG, tankfacts 1.023–1.026, reefapp ~1.026; retained the conservative reef-tank range 1.020–1.025. Minimum tank — LiveAquaria and AquariumDomain 30 gal (~114 L) for basic care with 90+ gal recommended for community settings, reefapp 100 L; biased upward to 150 L per the tank-size special rule given the territorial ambush-predator behavior and the need for ample rockwork to provide perches and a defensible territory.

Nomenclature note: 'Redbarred Hawkfish' is the FishBase-preferred common name; the trade overwhelmingly uses 'Blood Red Hawkfish' (LiveAquaria's marketing name) and 'Redbar / Banded Hawkfish' as alternates.

Secondary source swap: the originally designated Reef2Reef wiki thread returned HTTP 403; FishBase, reefapp.net, AquariumDomain and tankfacts.com were substituted for cross-verification.
breedingNotes: Has not been widely reported as captive-bred. As with other hawkfishes, this species is generally understood to be a protogynous sequential hermaphrodite that pair-spawns in haremic groups, with the male courting at dusk; eggs are pelagic and larvae have an extended planktonic phase, making rearing difficult outside of dedicated breeding setups.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Species product page. Provided scientific name (Cirrhitops fasciatus), family (Cirrhitidae), max size (5 in / 12.7 cm), minimum tank (30 gal), temperament (Aggressive), reef-safe (With Caution), care level (Easy), diet (carnivore — marine meats, frozen, live feeder shrimp), and water parameters (72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12). Cited as needing tank mates larger and at least semi-aggressive.

additional.notes: Fourth cross-check (color description and water parameters). Temperature 76–80°F, pH 7.8–8.4, SG 1.023–1.026, dKH 8–12. Confirmed appearance (bright red body, bluish-white facial freckles, faint vertical bars) and ambush-predator behavior.
